rulings.Case Title: LEIASA BECKHAM VS. KASLOFSKY & ASSOCIATES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 8, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANTS 1850 BRYANT LAND LLC, CHRISTOPHER FOLEY AND DOUGLAS ROSS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to July 14, 2022 for the moving party to comply with SFRL 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the operative complaint no later than June 15, 2022 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (Moving papers, opposition and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL YANCEY VS. SH DREAM, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 8, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED. Defendant Kuk dismissed on August 10, 2021. No order retaining jurisdiction on file as of that date. No evidence presented that any other defendants are signatories to the Settlement Agreement and no evidence of any breach of such agreement by other defendants. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order Granting Cross-Complainants Motion To Compel Responses To Written Disocvery, Deemed Matters Admitted, And Request For Monet Ary Sanctions; And Vik.Rant Punjs Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; [Proposed] Order.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 8, 2022 line 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT VIKRANT PUNJ'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE ORDER GRANTING CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY, DEEMED MATTERS ADMITTED, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is moot. January 31, 2022 Order is set aside on Court's own motion. Without a valid proof of service of the motion at the address on the record, the motion shouldn't have been considered and should have been taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR MCGUIGAN VS. MARIA LOUISA ZUNIGA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 8, 2022 line 9. DEFENDANT MARIA ZUNIGA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to reconcile allegations in paragraph 20 and the number of pages attached to the operative complaint. The Court notes that defendant and their counsel failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motions being taken off calendar/denied or treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
